title: Establishing the Select Committee to Defeat the Mexican Drug Cartels.
plain_summary: This bill creates a new special committee in the House of Representatives to study Mexican drug cartels. The committee can have up to twenty-one members who will investigate how these criminal groups work and figure out ways to stop them. The committee cannot make new laws, but they will study the problem and give their ideas to other committees that can make laws. They have to finish their work and give their final reports by the end of 2025 and 2026.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Rules.

sections.title: Section 1 Select Committee to Defeat the Mexican Drug Cartels
sections.text: The House of Representatives will create a special group called the Select Committee to Defeat the Mexican Drug Cartels. This committee will have no more than twenty-one members chosen by the Speaker of the House. Up to ten of these members will be chosen after talking with the minority leader. The Speaker will pick one member to be the chair or leader of the committee. If someone leaves the committee, they will be replaced the same way as the original choice. The committee must include at least one majority member from each of these five important House committees: Appropriations (which controls spending), Judiciary (which handles legal matters), Homeland Security, Armed Services (military), and Financial Services. The Speaker and minority leader can each pick one staff member to help them work with this committee. These staff members will have the same access to meetings and materials as regular committee staff and must follow the same security rules. This committee cannot write or vote on any laws. Its only job is to investigate and study Mexican drug cartels, including the networks that help these cartels operate and the efforts by the United States, Mexico, and other countries to stop them. The committee can hold public hearings if it chooses to do so. The committee will follow most of the same rules as regular House committees. Working on this committee will not count against limits on how many committees a member can serve on. The committee will hire its own staff following rules set by the Committee on House Administration. Staff from other House offices or federal agencies can be assigned to help this committee. The committee can hire outside consultants if the Speaker approves. The committee must report its findings and recommendations by December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-six. Policy recommendations must be given to the relevant regular committees by December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-five. Any proposed laws must be sent to the appropriate committees within sixty days of being approved by the Select Committee. All reports and proposals must be made available to the public within thirty days of completion. Reports will be unclassified but may include secret sections for classified or law enforcement sensitive information.
